name: Debian
description: Debian is a free, general-purpose operating system developed by a worldwide volunteer community. It is the foundation for hundreds of other distributions including Ubuntu.

name: Ubuntu
description: Ubuntu is a Debian-based Linux distribution developed by Canonical. It is one of the most popular desktop and server distributions worldwide, with regular LTS and interim releases.

name: Linux Mint
description: Linux Mint is a community-driven Ubuntu-based distribution focused on ease of use, an intuitive desktop, and out-of-the-box multimedia support.

name: Linux Mint Debian Edition (LMDE)
description: LMDE is Linux Mint's direct-from-Debian edition, intended as a hedge against the project's reliance on Ubuntu as its upstream base.

name: Pop!_OS
description: Pop!_OS is an Ubuntu-based distribution by System76 with a custom Rust-based COSMIC desktop environment, optimized for developers and creators.

name: Zorin OS
description: Zorin OS is an Ubuntu-based distribution designed to be a Windows/macOS replacement, with a polished desktop and a curated app ecosystem.

name: elementary OS
description: elementary OS is an Ubuntu-based distribution with a custom macOS-inspired Pantheon desktop environment, designed for elegance and simplicity.

name: Kubuntu
description: Kubuntu is the official KDE Plasma edition of Ubuntu. It packages Ubuntu with the KDE Plasma desktop and a curated set of KDE applications.

name: Xubuntu
description: Xubuntu is the official XFCE edition of Ubuntu, designed to be lightweight and efficient on older or lower-spec hardware.

name: Lubuntu
description: Lubuntu is the official LXQt edition of Ubuntu, focused on being a lightweight, energy-efficient distribution for older hardware.

name: Ubuntu MATE
description: Ubuntu MATE is the official MATE edition of Ubuntu, providing a traditional desktop experience inspired by GNOME 2.

name: Ubuntu Budgie
description: Ubuntu Budgie is the official Budgie desktop edition of Ubuntu, blending modern aesthetics with a familiar, lightweight desktop experience.

name: Ubuntu Studio
description: Ubuntu Studio is the official multimedia creation edition of Ubuntu, providing a curated suite of audio, video, graphics, and publishing tools.

name: Ubuntu Cinnamon
description: Ubuntu Cinnamon is a community flavour of Ubuntu that uses the Cinnamon desktop environment, developed primarily by the Linux Mint team.

name: Ubuntu Unity
description: Ubuntu Unity is a community flavour of Ubuntu reviving the Unity7 desktop environment, originally the default Ubuntu desktop from 2011-2017.

name: Edubuntu
description: Edubuntu is the official education edition of Ubuntu, providing educational software and a curated classroom-friendly experience.

name: Ubuntu Kylin
description: Ubuntu Kylin is the official Chinese edition of Ubuntu with a custom UKUI desktop, optimised for Chinese input methods and software.

name: UbuntuDDE
description: UbuntuDDE is an Ubuntu remix that ships the Deepin Desktop Environment (DDE), combining Ubuntu's stability with Deepin's polished UI.

name: Ubuntu Core
description: Ubuntu Core is a minimal, immutable Ubuntu variant built entirely from snap packages, designed for IoT and embedded devices.

name: Ubuntu Server
description: Ubuntu Server is the server edition of Ubuntu, optimised for cloud, edge, and on-premise workloads with no graphical desktop by default.

name: Vanilla OS
description: Vanilla OS is an immutable Ubuntu-based distribution that uses OCI images for atomic updates, with apx providing per-app containerised development environments.

name: BackBox
description: BackBox is an Ubuntu-based distribution focused on security assessment and penetration testing, providing a curated toolkit of security utilities.

name: Bodhi Linux
description: Bodhi Linux is a lightweight Ubuntu-based distribution that uses the Moksha desktop, a continuation of the Enlightenment E17 desktop.

name: Voyager
description: Voyager is an Ubuntu-based distribution with a heavily customised XFCE desktop, designed for a Mac-like and Windows-like feel in one system.

name: Linux Lite
description: Linux Lite is a lightweight Ubuntu-based distribution that provides an easy transition for Windows users on older or low-spec hardware.

name: Feren OS
description: Feren OS is an Ubuntu-based distribution with a custom KDE Plasma desktop designed to look and feel like Windows, easing the transition for new users.

name: Trisquel
description: Trisquel is a fully free Ubuntu-based distribution endorsed by the Free Software Foundation, using only FSF-approved packages and a Linux-libre kernel.

name: Nitrux
description: Nitrux is a Debian-based distribution with an immutable design, a customised KDE Plasma desktop, and the AppImage-centric NX software model.

name: Peppermint
description: Peppermint is a lightweight Debian-based distribution that integrates cloud applications (web apps) into the desktop via ICE.

name: MX Linux
description: MX Linux is a cooperative venture between antiX and MEPIS communities, using Debian Stable as a base with custom tools and a fast XFCE default.

name: antiX
description: antiX is a lightweight Debian-based distribution designed for old and low-resource computers, with no systemd by default and a choice of light window managers.

name: Devuan
description: Devuan is a fork of Debian that provides alternatives to systemd, offering users a choice of init systems (SysVinit, OpenRC, runit) and the freedom to choose.

name: Parrot OS
description: Parrot OS is a Debian-based distribution focused on security, penetration testing, and privacy, with editions for Home and Security.

name: Parrot Security
description: Parrot Security is the security-focused edition of Parrot OS, providing penetration testing, forensics, and reverse engineering tools.

name: Parrot Home
description: Parrot Home is the daily-driver edition of Parrot OS, providing privacy and development tools without the full security toolkit.

name: Kali Linux
description: Kali Linux is a Debian-derived distribution focused on penetration testing, security auditing, and digital forensics, with hundreds of security tools pre-installed.

name: Kali NetHunter
description: Kali NetHunter is a Kali Linux overlay for Android devices, providing a mobile penetration testing platform with kernel patches and additional tools.

name: Tails
description: Tails is a portable Debian-based live operating system that routes all traffic through Tor, designed to preserve privacy and anonymity.

name: Whonix
description: Whonix is a Debian-based, security-focused distribution that anonymises network traffic by routing everything through Tor, running as a gateway VM and a workstation VM.

name: deepin
description: deepin is a Debian-based distribution with a custom DDE desktop, developed in China, known for its elegant UI and integrated Deepin applications.

name: Raspberry Pi OS
description: Raspberry Pi OS (formerly Raspbian) is the official Debian-based distribution for the Raspberry Pi single-board computer, with a custom PIXEL desktop.

name: SpiralLinux
description: SpiralLinux is a Debian-based distribution providing curated desktop environments with the Calamares installer, intended as a friendlier Debian.

name: PureOS
description: PureOS is a Debian-based, FSF-endorsed distribution from Purism focused on privacy, security, and convergence, shipping the GNOME desktop.

name: Q4OS
description: Q4OS is a Debian-based distribution offering both modern KDE Plasma and the classic Trinity desktop, with a focus on speed and a Windows-like experience.

name: siduction
description: siduction is a Debian Sid-based distribution providing multiple desktop flavours, intended for advanced users who want a pure rolling-release Debian experience.

name: Knoppix
description: Knoppix is one of the original live CD Linux distributions, based on Debian, known for its strong hardware detection and rescue capabilities.

name: BunsenLabs
description: BunsenLabs Linux is a Debian-based distribution that continues the legacy of CrunchBang, providing a lightweight Openbox-based desktop.

name: CrunchBang
description: CrunchBang was a Debian-based distribution known for its lightweight Openbox desktop and minimalist philosophy; development ended in 2015 and was succeeded by BunsenLabs and CrunchBang++.

name: CrunchBang++
description: CrunchBang++ is a community continuation of the CrunchBang project, providing a lightweight Openbox-based Debian desktop.

name: AV Linux
description: AV Linux is a Debian-based distribution tailored for audio and video production, with a curated selection of creative tools and a low-latency kernel.

name: SparkyLinux
description: SparkyLinux is a lightweight Debian-based distribution offering rolling, semi-rolling, and stable editions, with a focus on a minimal but modern desktop experience.

name: Neptune
description: Neptune is a Debian-based distribution that focuses on delivering the latest stable KDE Plasma desktop, with a curated app selection and a clean look.

name: SolydXK
description: SolydXK is a Debian-based distribution providing two editions — SolydX (XFCE) and SolydK (KDE Plasma) — for desktop users seeking a friendly Debian.

name: Proxmox VE
description: Proxmox Virtual Environment is a Debian-based open-source server virtualization platform, providing KVM hypervisor and LXC container management through a web UI.

name: Endless OS
description: Endless OS is an immutable, Debian-based distribution that uses OSTree for atomic updates and ships a curated set of apps for first-time users.

name: Porteus Kiosk
description: Porteus Kiosk is a Debian-based locked-down operating system for public kiosks, designed to boot into a single full-screen web application and resist tampering.

name: Clonezilla
description: Clonezilla is a Debian-based partition and disk imaging/cloning tool, similar in spirit to Norton Ghost, with multicasting support for large deployments.

name: Pardus
description: Pardus is a Debian-based distribution developed by TÜBİTAK ULAKBİM in Turkey, providing both XFCE and GNOME editions with a focus on public-sector use.

name: Kali Linux Light
description: Kali Linux Light is a minimal-footprint version of Kali with a smaller set of tools, intended for use as a base for custom security distributions.

name: Kali Purple
description: Kali Purple is a defensive-security variant of Kali, focused on Security Operations Center (SOC) tooling, monitoring, and blue-team workflows.

name: Kali ARM
description: Kali ARM is the ARM port of Kali Linux, providing the same security tooling on Raspberry Pi, Pinebook Pro, and other ARM devices.

name: Parrot Mini
description: Parrot Mini is a lightweight edition of Parrot OS, intended for lower-spec machines and use as a base for custom builds.

name: Parrot for HTB
description: Parrot for HTB is a Parrot OS edition distributed to Hack The Box members, with the platform's VPN tools and preconfigured hacking environments.

name: Linux Lite
description: Linux Lite is a lightweight Ubuntu-based distribution that provides an easy transition for Windows users on older or lower-spec hardware.

name: LXLE
description: LXLE is a lightweight Ubuntu-based distribution using the LXDE desktop, designed to revive older computers with a familiar Windows-like experience.

name: MakuluLinux
description: MakuluLinux is a Debian-based distribution offering multiple desktop environments with a focus on stability, multimedia, and ease of use.

name: KDE neon
description: KDE neon is an Ubuntu-based distribution from the KDE community that ships the latest stable KDE Plasma, Frameworks, and applications on top of an Ubuntu LTS base.

name: KDE Linux
description: KDE Linux is an immutable, atomic KDE Plasma 6 desktop distribution by the KDE project, designed to provide a clean and stable KDE experience.

name: Netrunner
description: Netrunner was an Ubuntu-based distribution focused on a polished KDE Plasma experience, with curated apps and a custom artwork. Discontinued in 2018.

name: GalliumOS
description: GalliumOS is an Ubuntu-based distribution tailored for Chromebooks, replacing ChromeOS with a fast XFCE-based Linux desktop optimised for the hardware.

name: Ubuntu Touch
description: Ubuntu Touch is a mobile operating system for smartphones and tablets, originally by Canonical, now maintained by the UBports community.

name: Endless OS (e OS)
description: Endless OS, also known as e OS, is an immutable Debian-based distribution designed for first-time users with curated content packs and a streamlined experience.

name: Solus
description: Solus is an independent rolling-release distribution with the Budgie desktop and its own eopkg package manager. Originally started as a Debian fork, it is now built from scratch.

name: Linux From Scratch
package_manager: Manual / package managers can be added
description: Linux From Scratch (LFS) is a project that provides step-by-step instructions to build a custom Linux system entirely from source, by hand.
